nodes.label: Synapse v0.2 Roadmap
nodes.summary: v0.2 plan repositioning Synapse as observability + safety layer for any multi-agent stack

nodes.label: v0.2 Repositioning Thesis
nodes.rationale: v0.1 is a useful coordination primitive but not yet a complete coordination layer; v0.2 reframes Synapse as the missing observability + safety layer with conflict detection as the headline safety feature, not a coordination protocol that happens to come with a dashboard

nodes.label: v0.1 Findings
nodes.rationale: Three lessons from real 4-agent workloads: (1) original L2 router caught only simultaneous collisions, missed sequential same-resource overwrites — fixed in commit 217e7a7 with resolved_lookback_ms and stale_base_overwrite; (2) catching conflicts is not solving them — every CONFLICT was logged and ignored; (3) detection is structural, not semantic — semantically-equivalent writes to different scopes slip through

nodes.label: L2 Router Sequential-Overwrite Gap
nodes.rationale: Without resolved_lookback_ms, Synapse caught 0/5 expected collisions; with the fix, 5/5

nodes.label: Catching != Solving
nodes.rationale: Routed CONFLICTs were logged and ignored; integrations had no concept of what to do about them — motivates MergePolicy

nodes.label: Detection is Structural Not Semantic
nodes.rationale: Two agents writing different scope names that mean the same thing semantically slip through; coordinator already does belief-divergence detection but it isn't on the integration path

nodes.label: Envelopes Are Canonical
nodes.rationale: INTENTION/CONFLICT/RESOLUTION/BELIEF carry semantics OTel doesn't (blocking, gate_ms, expected_outcome, confidence, evidence). OTel is one I/O format among several.

nodes.label: Bring-Your-Own-LLM
nodes.rationale: Caller's agents already have an LLM. Synapse must never make a paid LLM call without explicit caller config. Internal reasoning reuses the user's existing model. If set_llm() is never called, Synapse runs in rules-only mode and gracefully degrades.

nodes.label: Audit-First Adoption
nodes.rationale: Read existing traces before writing live. Every modern agent framework already emits traces (OTel/LangSmith/W&B Weave/Phoenix/OpenInference/JSONL). Auditing them retroactively gives framework-agnostic value with zero integration code.

nodes.label: OpenInference-Flavored OTel as Substrate
nodes.rationale: Vendor-neutral, increasingly default; Arize/Phoenix already standardized tool-call span attributes. LangSmith and JSONL are extra importers.

nodes.label: Universal SDK + Opt-in Framework Adapters
nodes.rationale: One synapse.intend() context manager + auto-detect for OTel-emitting frameworks. Per-framework wrappers stay as sugar.

nodes.label: synapse.audit
nodes.summary: Trace importers (OTel/OpenInference, LangSmith, JSONL), scope-inference engine, HTML/JSON report generator
nodes.rationale: The wedge: works with any framework that emits any common trace format. Read-only entry point converts skeptics in 60s with their own data.

nodes.label: synapse.llm
nodes.summary: synapse.set_llm(), from_anthropic(), from_openai(), from_langchain(), from_litellm(), auto()
nodes.rationale: BYO-LLM: caller passes their existing client; Synapse uses it for scope inference, belief divergence, auto-merge.

nodes.label: synapse.intend
nodes.rationale: Universal SDK — works in any Python codebase regardless of framework. Replaces the per-framework wrapper as the recommended path.

nodes.label: synapse.install
nodes.rationale: One-line auto-instrumentation. OTel-emitting frameworks get Synapse for free; explicit framework adapters as opt-in sugar.

nodes.label: synapse CLI
nodes.summary: synapse audit, synapse up, synapse down, synapse install, synapse demo
nodes.rationale: 30-second adoption requires CLI parity with docker / pip, not docs.

nodes.label: MergePolicy
nodes.summary: MergePolicy ABC + four built-ins: redirect (default), wait, abort, auto_merge
nodes.rationale: Catching != solving. Policies are the bridge from we detected a CONFLICT to the multi-agent system actually does the right thing.

nodes.label: BELIEF Divergence on Integration Path
nodes.summary: BELIEF divergence runs on every BELIEF emission (not 30s tick); divergence signals route directly to other agents inboxes
nodes.rationale: Persona-2 (research crew with contradictory facts) needs sub-second feedback; 30s is useless for live coordination.

nodes.label: TS SDK (audit/intend/install/llm)
nodes.rationale: Polyglot stacks need polyglot SDKs. Same surface, same shapes.

nodes.label: runtime/router/worker.py changes
nodes.rationale: Add MergePolicy hook — when CONFLICT fires, dispatch to registered policy first; only route to inbox if policy returns inbox.

nodes.label: runtime/coordinator/agent.py changes
nodes.rationale: BELIEF divergence runs on every BELIEF emission (not 30s tick). Divergence signals route directly to other agents inboxes.

nodes.label: runtime/router/l3_semantic.py change
nodes.rationale: Read LLM from synapse.set_llm() instead of constructor arg — routes consolidate around one config point.

nodes.label: Remove Gemini default backend
nodes.rationale: All LLM use goes through synapse.set_llm(). No backend gets a default — caller decides.

nodes.label: Remove inline ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env reads
nodes.rationale: BYO-LLM means caller hands us a configured client, not a key.

nodes.label: Week 1 — Audit MVP
nodes.ship: synapse audit CLI + OTel/LangSmith/JSONL importers + scope inference + HTML report
nodes.success_metric: Stranger runs synapse audit on their LangSmith export, sees non-zero conflict count in <90s, with no Synapse runtime up

nodes.label: Week 2 — Universal SDK + LangGraph
nodes.success_metric: LangGraph user wires Synapse in 3 lines and sees live conflicts in their dashboard

nodes.label: Week 3 — synapse up + CrewAI/AutoGen/OpenAI adapters
nodes.ship: synapse up + dashboard re-positioning + CrewAI + AutoGen + OpenAI Assistants adapters
nodes.success_metric: Stranger lands on synapse.dev, runs synapse up, first multi-agent run rendered live in <5 min

nodes.label: Week 4 — MergePolicy + critical_scopes
nodes.ship: MergePolicy.{redirect, wait, abort, auto_merge} + critical_scopes selective enforcement
nodes.success_metric: Production user wires critical_scopes=[billing.*]; conflicting refund intentions hard-block instead of warn

nodes.label: Week 5 — BELIEF divergence on integration path
nodes.ship: BELIEF divergence on integration path + per-emission detection + auto-emit BELIEFs from tool results
nodes.success_metric: Data-analysis test catches revenue_formula disagreement via BELIEF even when two agents write to different scopes
nodes.norm_label: week 5 — belief divergence on integration path

nodes.label: Demo 1 — Instagram-clone with auto-merge
nodes.headline_result: git diff between no-Synapse run and with-Synapse run; with-Synapse models/user.py contains all 8 fields from db, api, and auth

nodes.label: Demo 2 — E-commerce checkout (selective enforcement on real money)
nodes.headline_result: Audit trail of every payment-related intention with sub-second hard-block decisions — production safety story
nodes.norm_label: demo 2 — e-commerce checkout (selective enforcement on real money)

nodes.label: Demo 3 — Marketing website (BELIEF divergence catches what scope can't)
nodes.scenario: Almost zero scope overlap; collisions are semantic (brand_color, tone)
nodes.headline_result: Rendered website screenshot from no-Synapse run vs Synapse run — consistent palette, consistent voice; demo for non-coding multi-agent work
nodes.norm_label: demo 3 — marketing website (belief divergence catches what scope can't)

nodes.label: Demo 4 — Data analysis pipeline extended with BELIEFs
nodes.scenario: data_cleaner vs analyst disagreement on revenue_formula (with vs without discount)
nodes.headline_result: Final report revenue numbers match across cleaner pre-aggregations and analyst roll-ups
nodes.norm_label: demo 4 — data analysis pipeline extended with beliefs

nodes.label: Demo 5 — Stress test 8 agents on real OSS bug backlog
nodes.scenario: Pull a public OSS repo with N open bugs; spawn 8 LangGraph crews, each on a different bug, all touching the same codebase
nodes.metric: PR success rate (bugs closed cleanly with no rework) with vs without Synapse
nodes.rationale: Empirical proof for the launch announcement
nodes.norm_label: demo 5 — stress test 8 agents on real oss bug backlog

nodes.label: AuditEvent schema (proposed)
nodes.fields: trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, agent_id, session_id, tool_name, tool_args, ts_start_ms, ts_end_ms, scope_inferred, status, state_diff?, raw

nodes.label: Audit runtime: in-process SQLite/DuckDB
nodes.rationale: Lean toward in-process SQLite/DuckDB for the audit so it runs without infra. Conflict-detection SQL is portable.

nodes.label: Auto-emit BELIEFs opt-in vs default
nodes.rationale: Lean opt-in (emit_beliefs_from_tool_results=True). Default-on risks surprising users with extra LLM calls.

nodes.label: Hosted Synapse paid convenience
nodes.rationale: Defer past v0.2. Focus on self-hosted flow first.

nodes.label: What v0.2 commits to NOT doing
items: Shipping more conflict kinds beyond v0.1
Full UI redesign before audit/install loop is proven
Enterprise features (SSO, audit logs, RBAC) before PMF
Trying to be the agent framework — Synapse wraps frameworks, never replaces them
nodes.norm_label: what v0.2 commits to not doing

nodes.label: Rules-only graceful degradation
nodes.rationale: If set_llm() is never called, L1 router (rules) and L2 router (SQL conflict detection) still work; L3 (semantic), belief divergence, scope inference fallback, and auto-merge become no-ops. No surprise charges on any account.

nodes.label: BYO-LLM two-role design
nodes.rationale: primary (user-facing decisions: auto-merge) + internal (high-frequency: scope inference, belief divergence). Internal defaults to cheapest model in the same provider family.

nodes.label: Week 1 day 1 first PR — audit MVP
items: audit/__init__.py with Importer ABC, OpenInferenceImporter, LangSmithImporter, JsonlImporter
cli/audit.py registered as synapse-audit console script
nodes.norm_label: week 1 day 1 first pr — audit mvp

nodes.label: Paperclip control plane
nodes.summary: Control plane for AI-agent companies; v1 implementation target. Mutable adapter registry seeded with built-ins; runtime validation on server routes; phase-1 branch feat/external-adapter-phase1

nodes.label: Paperclip phase-1 external-adapter work
nodes.approach: Mutable server + UI adapter registries (registerServerAdapter / registerUIAdapter), runtime adapter validation moved to server routes, open-ended adapterType validation in shared package, TDD-first

nodes.label: Hermes agent (ACP)
nodes.summary: Agent with ACP tool-call dispatch; TOOL_KIND_MAP maps hermes tool names to ACP ToolKind (read/edit/search/execute/fetch/think/other); _POLISHED_TOOLS set

nodes.summary: Personal AI assistant on user devices; multi-channel (WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Discord/...); plugin/extension pattern; openclaw onboard CLI

nodes.label: Synapse Architecture and Execution Plan v1.0
nodes.author: Aadit Rajgor
nodes.norm_label: synapse architecture and execution plan v1.0

nodes.label: Synapse thesis: HTTP for agent collaboration
nodes.rationale: Agent runtime (turn-taking, tool use, retries) is solved by CrewAI/LangGraph/AutoGen/raw SDKs. What does not exist is a coordination layer that lets these runtimes talk to each other, share state, and avoid stepping on each other's work. Synapse = HTTP for agent collaboration, not Express.js.
nodes.norm_label: synapse thesis: http for agent collaboration

nodes.label: Fail-open rule
nodes.rationale: If Synapse goes down, agents continue working without coordination, log a warning, and reconcile when the bus recovers. Non-negotiable.

nodes.label: Happy-path latency <50ms
nodes.rationale: Slow coordination = no adoption

nodes.label: Protocol (7 message types)
nodes.tech: JSON Schema, ULIDs, parent_msg_id causality, session_id multi-tenancy

nodes.label: Inference Adapter Layer
nodes.rationale: Signal injection works differently across backends; SDK abstracts both behind unified adapter interface

nodes.label: Append-and-continue (not cancel-and-restart)
nodes.rationale: Cancel-and-restart discards work; append-and-continue preserves the KV cache and only adds new tokens. Forward-causal attention means earlier K/V cannot be retroactively edited but new context can be appended. Effective overhead drops from ~1.3x to ~1.05x.

nodes.label: Three urgency tiers for routing
nodes.tiers: Low (FYI: inbox at decision point) / Medium (Native+Local inject freely; Hosted batch+inject if relevance>0.8) / High (inject immediately on all)

nodes.label: ADR-1 JSON Schema for protocol
nodes.rationale: Developer ergonomics and operational debuggability matter more than wire size at <10k msg/sec/session

nodes.label: ADR-2 Redis Streams as message bus
nodes.rationale: Sub-millisecond latency, built-in persistence with retention, consumer groups, single operational dependency
nodes.norm_label: adr-2 redis streams as message bus

nodes.label: ADR-3 Postgres + JSONB for state graph
nodes.rationale: Scope-overlap and key-divergence queries trivial in SQL; GIN indexes on text[] make scope overlap fast; one database not two
nodes.norm_label: adr-3 postgres + jsonb for state graph

nodes.label: ADR-4 Three-tier filtering rules->SQL->LLM
nodes.rationale: 95% of routing decisions can be made by L1+L2 (free, sub-10ms). L3 catches the subtle 5% but at 100-500ms and real cost.

nodes.label: ADR-5 Event-driven coordinator
nodes.rationale: Polling either wastes tokens or misses fast-evolving situations; event-driven scales naturally

nodes.label: ADR-6 Decorator-based SDK
nodes.rationale: Works with any agent runtime (LangGraph nodes, CrewAI tasks, raw functions). Sidesteps inheritance/composition debate.

nodes.label: ADR-7 Backend selection at agent registration
nodes.rationale: Per-call selection is unpredictable; per-agent matches reality (agent has a model, model has a host)
nodes.norm_label: adr-7 backend selection at agent registration

nodes.label: ADR-8 Cost telemetry as first-class signal
nodes.rationale: Without COST_REPORT, routing thresholds cannot be tuned; data is essential to making the smart layer adaptive
nodes.norm_label: adr-8 cost telemetry as first-class signal

nodes.label: Phase 0 — Pre-Code Decisions
nodes.deliverable: Public repo with README, LICENSE, /spec/protocol-v1.0/*.schema.json, /spec/adapter.md, Docker Compose stub. Zero application code.

nodes.label: Phase 2 (Weeks 3-4) — Real Coordination
nodes.acceptance: Two agents refactoring auth/middleware.py simultaneously; one claims first, second pivots; L1+L2 p99 <10ms with 100 active intentions

nodes.label: Phase 3 (Weeks 5-6) — Mid-Stream Injection
nodes.acceptance: Same script across all three backends; cost report ~1.05x/~1.08x/~1.15x overhead

nodes.label: Phase 4 (Weeks 7-8) — Intelligence Layer
nodes.acceptance: Coordinator detects belief divergence; total coordination cost <$0.10 for 10-min session

nodes.label: Phase 5 (Weeks 9-10) — Smart Router
nodes.acceptance: L3 decisions match human judgment >80%; L3 cost <5% of total session cost

nodes.label: Phase 6 (Weeks 11-12) — Adoption Surface
nodes.acceptance: Install + bench + plug into CrewAI + UI working in <15 min

nodes.label: Multi-tenant isolation modes
nodes.modes: process (own engine per agent) | request_id (shared engine, per-call validation) | none (single-tenant only). v1 ships process only; request_id in v1.1.
nodes.rationale: Adapter rejects cross-tenant KV access at the boundary. Router and coordinator never see KV cache directly.

nodes.label: Reasoning-model handling
nodes.rationale: Reasoning models (o1, o3, Claude extended thinking) get is_reasoning_model:true with supports_midstream_inject:false during thinking. Signals queue silently and inject at boundary transitions.

nodes.label: One-time setup (docker compose up, pip install -e sdk-python)
nodes.norm_label: one-time setup (docker compose up, pip install -e sdk-python)

nodes.label: L2 conflict detection (SQL + Python scope matcher)
nodes.norm_label: l2 conflict detection (sql + python scope matcher)

nodes.norm_label: self-terminating per-session task bounds memory rationale

nodes.norm_label: cors wide-open in dev; restrict in production

attributes.description: Locked protocol spec; eight message types over ULID-keyed envelope
attributes.rationale: Protocol is the durable contract; runtime/SDK/adapters are implementations of it

attributes.description: Wrapper around every message; ULID-keyed
THOUGHT, INTENTION, PIVOT, BELIEF, BLOCK, CONFLICT, RESOLUTION, COST_REPORT

attributes.when_emitted: Before any tool call or major step
attributes.note: Most important message; drives all conflict detection

attributes.default_routing: Routed to the agent whose intention triggered it

attributes.rationale: BLOCK is agent-initiated 'I am stuck, please help'; CONFLICT is runtime-initiated 'router detected your intention collides with another agent's claim'

attributes.description: All implementations MUST validate every message against schema before publishing; invalid messages dropped + logged
Single most important interface in Synapse; uniform shape over any LLM backend
attributes.rationale: SDK and runtime must drive any backend (vLLM, Ollama, hosted) identically

attributes.description: native / local_api / hosted backend tiers

attributes.fields: backend_id, tier, supports_midstream_inject, supports_partial_preservation, is_reasoning_model, prompt_cache_available, avg_overhead_per_signal, multi_tenant_isolation

attributes.description: Opaque handle to in-flight generation
Begin a streaming generation
Caller may stop iterating without cancelling underlying request
Append context mid-generation and resume; the KEY method
attributes.tier_behavior: Native: KV append; Local-API: resume from exposed tokens; Hosted: cancel + cached restart with prefix + partial output + injection

attributes.description: Cancel in-flight generation; returns whatever was generated; empty string if no partial preservation
True if inject_and_continue works during active generation
True if partial output preserved on cancel; affects routing economics — backends without preservation pay full restart cost so router raises injection thresholds
Tokens tagged is_thinking; SDK queues signals while thinking==true; injection happens at thinking-end boundary or response complete; THOUGHT emitted as boundary marker
attributes.implication: decision points per-turn (10-60s), not per-token; mid-thinking interruption not supported in v1

attributes.purpose: drives coordinator's adaptive routing thresholds + dev-facing cost dashboard

attributes.handling: SDK falls back to no-coordination mode for affected agent until backend recovers

attributes.description: inject_and_continue called on handle that just finished naturally; return completed result without error
Defensive raise when backend asked to do unsupported op (e.g., inject during reasoning thinking)
Dotted path with optional read/write modifiers and glob wildcards
attributes.ebnf: scope := segment ('.' segment)* [':' modifier]; segment := name | wildcard; wildcard := '*' | '**'; modifier := r | w | rw

attributes.description: Two scopes match if patterns intersect; conflict if match AND at least one is write AND held by different agents
attributes.rationale: 'Just check string match' is too simple — db.users.schema and db.users.email should not always conflict, but db.users.* should match both

attributes.description: Matches one segment; auth.* matches auth.middleware but not auth.middleware.config
Matches any depth; db.users.** matches db.users.schema and db.users.email.index

attributes.rules: r/r=no conflict; r/w or w/r=conflict; w/w=conflict; rw treated as w
attributes.rationale: Read-only intentions useful — querying agents shouldn't block other queries; only writes serialize

attributes.description: If INTENTION sets blocks_others pattern, any matching intention (read or write) becomes CONFLICT of kind exclusive_claim
Agent's own intentions never conflict with each other; allows holding multiple active intentions

attributes.conflict: scopes intersect, at least one writes -> CONFLICT message
attributes.dependency: B's INTENTION declares dependency on A's pending RESOLUTION; no CONFLICT, B can wait
attributes.v1_0_status: conflict only; depends_on lands in v1.1

attributes.stage1: Postgres GIN index over scope::text[] using && (array overlap) — sub-millisecond at thousands of intentions
attributes.stage2: Pure-Python pattern resolver with wildcard expansion + modifier checks; no LLM in conflict path
attributes.edge_case: ** wildcards stored expanded when known; regex fallback bounded by per-session active intentions (<100)

attributes.items: path-based scopes with structural understanding; time-bounded claims (scope:w@5m); probabilistic dependencies; structural diff awareness

attributes.description: Multi-agent product (coding assistant, research workflow, agentic SaaS)

attributes.purpose: real-time intent broadcasting, conflict detection, pivot signaling, observability inside one work session
connecting models to tools, files, databases, APIs

attributes.backends: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, vLLM, Ollama, llama.cpp

attributes.additions: 1) Pre-action signaling (intentions broadcast before tool calls), 2) Built-in conflict detection (routed signals not just logs), 3) Bidirectional injection (runtime injects signals back into agents)

attributes.description: Database gives 'what is currently true'; Synapse gives 'what is about to happen' — the few hundred ms between agent deciding to act and action firing
attributes.rationale: That window is invisible to any state-snapshot system

attributes.flow: Agent emits INTENTION declaring scope before MCP tool call; router checks; if clear proceed, if conflict pivot; MCP unaware of Synapse
Wrap each LangGraph node with @agent.intention decorator; parallel branches touching shared state get auto conflict signals; sequential branches no overhead
Two orgs' agents discover/connect via A2A; inside resulting task both register with shared Synapse session; A2A handles trust boundary, Synapse handles operational tempo

attributes.items: Protocol (8 types JSON Schema ULID envelope); Redis Streams bus; Postgres+JSONB GIN-indexed scope[]; 3-layer router (L1 rules, L2 SQL, L3 Haiku); Sonnet-class coordinator event-driven; Python SDK (TS month 8) decorator-based; 3-tier adapter; Next.js+WS observability UI; synapse bench CLI

attributes.rationale: Preserves KV cache, ~1.05x overhead on native backends — vs cancel-and-restart

attributes.behavior: consume at decision points / inject mid-stream / trigger pre-execution gates

attributes.description: Hosted-agent injection thresholds raised dynamically based on COST_REPORT telemetry

attributes.context: Earlier draft referenced CONFLICT in INTENTION schema description but didn't define it as payload type; spec inconsistency caught during external review and resolved before any implementation depended on it

attributes.change: From: Anthropic adapter; To: mocked-backend end-to-end demo. Real adapter slips to Phase 2 to accelerate proof-of-protocol

nodes.label: Will revisit: vLLM KV append API stability

attributes.description: Track upstream changes

nodes.norm_label: will revisit: vllm kv append api stability

nodes.label: Will revisit: L3 router cost at scale

attributes.description: May need tiered Haiku/rules hybrid as session volume grows

nodes.norm_label: will revisit: l3 router cost at scale

attributes.description: Track provider API evolution

attributes.scope: envelope schemas, agent_registration, intention, thought, pivot, belief, block, conflict, resolution, cost_report, adapter contract, conflict semantics

attributes.backward_compatible: add optional fields, x- prefixed enums/types, promote x- via minor version
attributes.incompatible_requires_2_0: remove/rename fields, change types, change required-ness, reorder required, modify MessageType semantics, change envelope shape

attributes.purpose: reference validator for envelopes against schema before bus publish

attributes.rationale: v1.0 envelope types carry semantics OTel spans do not (blocking, gate_ms, expected_outcome, confidence, evidence, suggested_resolution); flattening would be lossy. OTel/LangSmith/JSONL relegated to I/O formats only.
attributes.io_input_role: trace importers normalize external traces into envelopes for audit and live ingestion
attributes.io_output_role: envelope-to-OTel exporters feed Phoenix, Arize, Datadog

attributes.rationale: Synapse never makes a paid LLM call without explicit consent; reuses caller's existing LLM client for scope inference, belief divergence, auto-merge, L3 semantic routing. Guarantees no surprise charges.
attributes.rules_only_fallback: L1+L2 work; L3, BELIEF divergence, auto-merge become no-op; audit scope inference covers ~70% via rules

attributes.rationale: First user interaction is read-only on existing trace data; produces compelling artifact (their own data showing waste numbers) that converts skeptics in 60 seconds. Live integration is upgrade path.
Open standard with growing adoption (LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI SDK, Anthropic SDK, AutoGen); standardizes tool-call span attributes Synapse needs; vendor-neutral; built on OTel.
Adds vendor dependency to vendor-neutral library; surprise cost/latency; local-only adopters (Ollama, vLLM) explicitly do not want network LLM call.

nodes.label: Alt B: OTel as canonical wire format

attributes.rationale: OTel spans don't carry protocol semantics (blocking, expected_outcome, etc.); forces lossy translation; couples Synapse evolution to OTel's.

nodes.norm_label: alt b: otel as canonical wire format

attributes.rationale: Live integration has 100x surface area (every framework hot loop); audit artifact more compelling to skeptics (their own data); audit code reusable as live-trace decoders.
Every CONFLICT routed was logged and ignored; integrations had no concept of how to act on a conflict.

attributes.example: cleaner.revenue_formula vs analyst.revenue_formula in different files slipped through

attributes.right_primitive: explicit policies (wait/abort/redirect), with auto-merge as opt-in

attributes.rationale: Round-trips gate every agent loop; exposes tool-call payloads to vendors; SLA dependency. Self-hosted via docker compose is the correct default.
Every agent framework already emits traces in some standard format; consuming traces (audit) gets framework-agnosticism for free.

attributes.items: sdk-python/synapse/audit/, cli/audit.py, 10 fixture trace files (LangGraph/CrewAI/AutoGen/raw OpenAI/Anthropic), tests, README quickstart

attributes.purpose: wraps an individual Hermes tool call with INTENTION + gate + RESOLUTION
installs runtime hooks into Hermes session
attributes.params: bus, state, session_id, agent_id, gate_ms, fail_on_conflict

attributes.purpose: registers Hermes agent with Synapse (mentioned in chunk task brief; implied by smoke-output 'agents registered: 1')

attributes.write_execute_examples: write_file, patch, terminal, execute_code, delegate_task, browser_click

attributes.examples: write_file path -> repo.fs.<path>:w; terminal -> repo.shell:w; browser_navigate url -> repo.browser.<sanitized>:w; delegate_task -> hermes.subagent.<id>:w

attributes.rationale: delegate_task spawns a subagent; INTENTION on hermes.subagent.<id>:w lets L2 catch cross-subagent collisions even when subagents work in parallel.

attributes.verification: adapter-tested via inline Modal smoke; structurally identical to Paperclip wrapper
attributes.modes: wrap existing extension OR standalone Synapse extension

attributes.purpose: wraps an existing OpenClaw extension so write tools go through Synapse coordination
attributes.params: extension, {bus, sessionId, agentId, scopeFromCall, gateMs, failOnConflict, isWriteTool, synapseBackend}

attributes.rationale: checks tool.isWrite first then name-based heuristic for write/edit/patch/delete/create/update/execute/run/send/post/publish/deploy/commit

attributes.framework_thesis: agents-as-employees with org charts, budgets, and goals
attributes.verification: adapter-tested via vitest (5/5) plus inline-mock Modal smoke

attributes.purpose: wraps a Paperclip server adapter to emit INTENTION/RESOLUTION/COST_REPORT around each task dispatch
attributes.params: innerAdapter, {bus, sessionId, scopeFromTask, failOnConflict (default true), gateMs (default 50), synapseBackend}

nodes.label: Paperclip org-level vs Synapse operational-level coordination

attributes.paperclip_role: org chart: reporting structures, budgets, escalation paths, ownership declarations
attributes.synapse_role: operational: intention broadcasting, scope conflict detection, mid-stream pivots; enforces ownership at runtime

attributes.coverage: INTENTION+RESOLUTION+COST_REPORT clean dispatch, CONFLICT->AdapterError, scopeFromTask, failure outcome, mock convenience

attributes.purpose: register caller's existing LLM client for Synapse internal reasoning

attributes.input: any common trace format export (langsmith-export.json, etc.)
attributes.output: HTML report counting silent conflicts and waste tokens

attributes.adopters: LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI SDK, Anthropic SDK, AutoGen

attributes.description: Wrap framework tool/task dispatch site, emit INTENTION before action, listen for CONFLICT during gate window, emit RESOLUTION after action completes
